audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Jacob's illness, Joseph's sons, adoption of Ephraim/Manasseh, Rachel memory, crossed hands, younger-over-older blessing, and promise of return.

verses.restored: After these things Joseph was told, Look, your father is sick. He took with him his two sons, Manasseh and Ephraim.
Jacob was told, Look, your son Joseph has come to you. Israel strengthened himself and sat up on the bed.
Jacob said to Joseph, El Shaddai appeared to me at Luz in the land of Canaan and blessed me.
He said to me, Look, I will make you fruitful and multiply you, make you an assembly of peoples, and give this land to your seed after you as an everlasting possession.
Now your two sons born to you in Egypt before I came to you in Egypt are mine; Ephraim and Manasseh shall be mine, like Reuben and Simeon.
Your offspring whom you father after them shall be yours; they shall be called by the name of their brothers in their inheritance.

As for me, when I came from Paddan, Rachel died by me in the land of Canaan on the way, while there was still some distance to Ephrath, and I buried her there on the way to Ephrath, that is Bethlehem.
Israel saw Joseph’s sons and said, Who are these?
Joseph said to his father, They are my sons whom the Divine has given me here. He said, Please bring them to me, and I will bless them.
Israel’s eyes were heavy from age; he could not see. Joseph brought them near to him, and he kissed them and embraced them.
Israel said to Joseph, I had not thought to see your face, and look, the Divine has also shown me your seed.
Joseph brought them out from between his knees and bowed with his face to the earth.
Joseph took both of them, Ephraim in his right hand toward Israel’s left, and Manasseh in his left hand toward Israel’s right, and brought them near.
Israel stretched out his right hand and placed it on Ephraim’s head, though he was the younger, and his left hand on Manasseh’s head, crossing his hands, though Manasseh was the firstborn.
He blessed Joseph and said, The Divine before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac walked, the Divine who has shepherded me all my life to this day,

the messenger who redeemed me from all evil, bless the boys. Let my name be called upon them, and the name of my fathers Abraham and Isaac, and let them multiply like fish into abundance in the midst of the earth.
Joseph saw that his father placed his right hand on Ephraim’s head, and it was evil in his eyes. He took his father’s hand to remove it from Ephraim’s head to Manasseh’s head.
Joseph said to his father, Not so, my father, for this one is the firstborn; put your right hand on his head.

His father refused and said, I know, my son, I know. He too shall become a people, and he too shall be great; nevertheless his younger brother shall be greater than he, and his seed shall become the fullness of nations.
He blessed them that day, saying, By you Israel shall bless, saying, May the Divine make you like Ephraim and like Manasseh. So he set Ephraim before Manasseh.
Israel said to Joseph, Look, I am dying, but the Divine will be with you and return you to the land of your fathers.
I give you one shoulder/portion above your brothers, which I took from the hand of the Amorite with my sword and with my bow.
